title: Questlog
description: Adds a quest system designed to make info conveyance in modpacks far more intuitive!
body:
![](https://media.forgecdn.net/attachments/description/1202066/description_4b293d5f-4a73-4016-8144-2e34dbcd1008.png)

![](https://media.forgecdn.net/attachments/description/1202066/description_b075f5b1-2af2-46a2-97f5-ed04a749d33d.png)

**Questlog** is a mod designed to add… Well, _quests_ to your game!

This mod is _entirely JSON-driven. You will_ need to learn how to write JSON files and put them in the questlog folder in the config to make use of it.

The biggest question on your mind is probably something like: "... Why not just use FTBQuests?"

![](https://media.forgecdn.net/attachments/description/1202066/description_9e24f0a0-6c85-4f19-9a88-ac26537da179.png)

Well! Unlike FTBQuests, which presents its quests in sprawling spreadsheets, _this_ mod's quests are presented in a little book/checklist-style format, and are directly highlighted via notification badges when unlocked from the inventory GUI! 

![](https://media.forgecdn.net/attachments/1611/926/2026-04-03_09-24-53-png.png)

The presentation is both far more vanilla-friendly, yet also dynamic enough to be stylised in whatever way you see fit! This format was directly inspired by [Elder Scrolls: Oblivion](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=N6hVmn9FM7o)'s quest system.

Although designed to fill in the gaps I feel are present in FTBQuests, I could easily see this mod being used in conjunction with it, as it fulfils different niches than that mod, even if they're similar. 

Whilst FBT is better for delivering numerous _smaller tasks_ (i.e. fetch-quests), Questlog is better for delivering information and highlighting more complex goals. It also just has a fair few advantages over FTB, too...

![](https://media.forgecdn.net/attachments/description/1202066/description_7131e988-6c46-4ff1-a500-9880efc0afb8.png)

The mod was conceptualised and is designed around the principle of **conveyance.** Creators can write quests so that when they trigger (or are completed), they display either their own toast panels and notification badges, **or** they outright _pop-up_ in your face directly, so you can never miss an important piece of info.

Every art asset of the mod has been scrupulously designed so as to be majorly alterable via **resource packs**, so you can include Questlog in all manner of modpacks without it feeling out of place.

By default, the mod has no content. You have to add your own via writing quests and adding them t!

![](https://media.forgecdn.net/attachments/description/1202066/description_e107db13-3af8-4ba1-89a9-70c952e76f27.png)

Check out the wiki for info on how to use Questlog!   
[https://moddedmc.wiki/en/project/questlog/latest/docs](https://moddedmc.wiki/en/project/questlog/latest/docs)

You can also find example Quests to use as reference here:   
[https://github.com/infernalstudios/Questlog/tree/1.20.1/examples/questlog](https://github.com/infernalstudios/Questlog/tree/1.20.1/examples/questlog)

After examining that, check out this Questlog generator to more easily create your own quests:  
[https://evanbowness.dev/questlog.html](https://evanbowness.dev/questlog.html)

![](https://media.forgecdn.net/attachments/description/1202066/description_0814af2e-19f6-4fd1-9c51-46d3ba649d3b.png)

Check out the mod in action in the [Resurvival](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/modpacks/resurvival) modpack, which I would suggest examining as a template to help you write your own quests and make your own resource packs!

Hope this helps you make better modpacks! :D

![](https://media.forgecdn.net/attachments/description/1202066/description_1f76eec4-2ccf-43b5-ab2a-f32159b7a609.png)

Got a feature suggestion or an issue? Let us know on our GitHub! [https://github.com/infernalstudios/Questlog/issues](https://github.com/infernalstudios/Questlog/issues)

Also, show us what you do with the mod in our Discord! [https://discord.gg/UYGSn94](https://discord.gg/UYGSn94)

Wanna see the mod in action? _Can't read?_  
Don't worry, I've got you covered!! Check it out in action below!
